title: veronica incana subsp. hololeuca
intro: Veronica incana subsp. hololeuca, also known as the woody veronica, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic and astringent, making it useful in treating urinary tract issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of Veronica incana subsp. hololeuca include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Veronica incana subsp. hololeuca is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
